ledCommentary.commentary: Preferred option for 320 x 160 module sizes; includes hub card. Slim, good flatness, but cut cabinets can be relatively expensive

Use for 320 x 160 applications where client is price sensitive, or where cabinet sizes are awkward/expensive with BM-PRO. Deeper and includes cables compared to BM-PRO; but uplift for cut cabinets significantly lower in cost.
Preferred option for 250 x 250 module sizes; includes hub card. Slim, good flatness, fast install; low power use
LEDFul option for 250 x 250mm - use where store has other screens from LEDFul; or 33mm depth (vs 44mm for WallPad) is useful. Because of slimness, harder to cable that WallPad

Steel cabinets, so low precision. This makes assembling multiple cabinets difficult to align. Use this product for tickers (one or two modules hig) or where 1 cabinet x 2 to 3 cabinets (high or wide) rather than multi dimensional and client is price sensitive. Flatness not as good as BM, nor alignment. Max P1.8, unless only a single cabinet in which case can use for higher resolutions. Max cabinet width 1600mm. Has advantage of optionally including edge trim as part of cabinet (at extra cost) where this is needed. While default is 99mm deep, can be made slimmer (down to 60mm) where necessary (at additional cost for differen power supplies). There are many more module sizes available so if there are specific needs speak to LEDFul about options.
Only use where these dimensions are required (pure 16:9 or specific width).
Old product; used only for specific dimensions - for smaller screens (e.g.Peter Alexander 1080 x 1440mm) recommended instead to us an IF based on same size modules
Use IF where smallish or a single column; BM-Rear is a good option (but must be 640x480 (or rotated) cabinets) if this works for dimensions, else HI (rental cabinets).

These are the go-to option for smaller screens as they are cost effective and front service is strightforward for modules and power supplies. Sizes can be easily adjusted because cabinets are steel. Steel cabinet (less precise, more prone to corrosion) and modules themselves aren't waterproof from behind so reliant on waterproofing the cabinet. To be avoided for larger screens (~>8sqm) due to more difficult install.

Aluminium cabinet; more precise, better for extreme weather (snow etc. IP67) than FA. Modules are waterproof but have plastic back. Module maintenance from the front is easy, but cards /power is awkward if access is difficult as whole spine needs to be removed. Preferred option for dual service environments where value is important.

Aluminium backed modules, premium product; preferred for less largeraccessible/ higher uptime. can be tweaked for lower power use / higher brightness / goldwire if required. Note FS-PRO at less than P4 and more than P4    havequite different cabinet structures
Default, old 'skeleton' style LED. Must be hung (can be hung from a frame relatively easily) Fragile, client must be made aware of risk of damage (e.g. from cleaners)

Go-to for Mesh LED. Can be stuck to either front or rear of glass (needs to be ordered as such). Modules can be cut to non-standard sizes but you need to pay for full module. Note that 1500mm modules can't be used in conjunction with smaller modules. Can be installed with control rails top/botton or left right; or hung
LEDFul equivalent of Muxwave. Limited use by Seen so far; but seems OK with similar constraints
Has not been used by Seen
Similar to IF but based on silicon modules so can be curved. Many other modules sizes are available where needed
These are used for rental applications, and also for rear service install applications
Fixed cabinet sizes (500x500 or 500 x 1000). Front service is not really front service at high resolutions
X and T are same product jsut  500x500 or 500 x 1000 cabinet sizes. LEDFul proprietary cabinet design
